title: Thunderstorms
content:
My mind has thunderstorms,
     That brood for heavy hours:
    Until they rain me words,
     My thoughts are drooping flowers
    And sulkin

title: A Fleeting Passion
content:
Thou shalt not laugh, thou shalt not romp,
     Let's grimly kiss with bated breath;
    As quietly and solemnly
     As Life when it is kissin

title: The Helpless
content:
Those poor, heartbroken wretches, doomed
     To hear at night the clocks' hard tones;

title: Oh, Sweet Content!
The Villain
content:

title: The Child And The Mariner
A May Morning
The White Cascade
Return To Nature
content:
My song is of that city which
    Has men too poor and men too rich;
    Where some are sick, too richly fed,
    While others take the sparrow

title: Francis Thompson
content:
Thou hadst no home, and thou couldst see
     In every street the windows' light:

title: The Starved
content:
My little Lamb, what is amiss?
    If there was milk in mother's kiss,
    You would not look as white as this.

    The wolf of Hunger, it is

title: Joy Supreme
content:
The birds are pirates of her notes,
     The blossoms steal her face's light;
    The stars in ambush lie all day,
     To take her glances for

title: The Two Lives
content:
Now how could I, with gold to spare,
     Who know the harlot's arms, and wine,
    Sit in this green field all alone,
     If Nature was not t

title: Laughing Rose
content:
If I were gusty April now,
     How I would blow at laughing Rose;
    I'd make her ribbons slip their knots,
     And all her hair come loose.

title: Love's Caution
content:
Tell them, when you are home again,
        How warm the air was now;
    How silent were the birds and leaves,
        And of the moon's full

title: Smiles
Seeking Joy
content:
Joy, how I sought thee!
    Silver I spent and gold,
    On the pleasures of this world,
        In splendid garments clad;
    The wine I dra

title: Birds
content:
When our two souls have left this mortal clay
     And, seeking mine, you think that mine is lost -
    Look for me first in that Elysian glade

title: The Fog
content:
I saw the fog grow thick,
     Which soon made blind my ken;
    It made tall men of boys,
     And giants of tall men.

    It clutched my th

title: The Heap Of Rags
content:
One night when I went down
    Thames' side, in London Town,
    A heap of rags saw I,
    And sat me down close by.
    That thing could sh

title: Dream Tragedies
content:

    In store, and ofttimes make us know;

title: Life Is Jolly
My Youth
Strong Moments
content:
Sometimes I hear fine ladies sing,
     Sometimes I smoke and drink with men;
    Sometimes I play at games of cards, 
     Judge me to be no s

title: Raptures
content:
Sing for the sun your lyric, lark,
     Of twice ten thousand notes;
    Sing for the moon, you nightingales,
     Whose light shall kiss your

title: The Captive Lion
content:
Thou that in fury with thy knotted tail
    Hast made this iron floor thy beaten drum;
    That now in silence walkst thy little space,

title: Who I Know
content:
I do not know his grace the Duke,
     Outside whose gilded gate there died
    Of want a feeble, poor old man,
     With but his shadow at his

title: Wasted Hours
content:
How many buds in this warm light
        Have burst out laughing into leaves!
    And shall a day like this be gone
        Before I seek the w

title: A Woman's Charms
content:
My purse is yours, Sweet Heart, for I
    Can count no coins with you close by;
    I scorn like sailors them, when they
    Have drawn on shor

title: A Great Time
content:
Sweet Chance, that led my steps abroad,
     Beyond the town, where wild flowers grow -
    A rainbow and a cuckoo, Lord,
     How rich and gre

title: Days Too Short
content:
When primroses are out in Spring,
     And small, blue violets come between;
     When merry birds sing on boughs green,
    And rills, as so

title: An Early Love
content:
Ah, sweet young blood, that makes the heart
     So full of joy, and light,
    That dying children dance with it
     From early morn till nig

title: The Bird-Man
content:
Man is a bird:
     He rises on fine wings
    Into the Heaven's clear light;
     He flies away and sings, 
    There's music in his flight.

title: England
content:
We have no grass locked up in ice so fast
    That cattle cut their faces and at last,
    When it is reached, must lie them down and starve,

title: Mad Poll
content:
There goes mad Poll, dressed in wild flowers,
     Poor, crazy Poll, now old and wan;
    Her hair all down, like any child:
     She swings he

title: Christmas
content:
Christmas has come, let's eat and drink, 
    This is no time to sit and think;
    Farewell to study, books and pen,
    And welcome to all ki

title: A Bird's Anger
Cowslips And Larks
content:
I hear it said yon land is poor,
    In spite of those rich cowslips there -
    And all the singing larks it shoots
    To heaven from the cow

title: The Hawk
content:
Thou dost not fly, thou art not perched,
     The air is all around:
    What is it that can keep thee set,
     From falling to the ground?

title: The Bell
content:
It is the bell of death I hear,
    Which tells me my own time is near,
    When I must join those quiet souls
    Where nothing lives but worm

title: Sweet Birds, I Come
content:
The bird that now
     On bush and tree,
    Near leaves so green
     Looks down to see
    Flowers looking up, 
     He either sings
    I

title: Love And The Muse
The Lonely Dreamer
The Moon
content:
Thy beauty haunts me heart and soul,
     Oh thou fair Moon, so close and bright;
    Thy beauty makes me like the child
     That cries aloud

title: Easter
content:
What exultations in my mind,
    From the love-bite of this Easter wind!
    My head thrown back, my face doth shine
    Like yonder Sun's, but

title: Lovely Dames
Heigh Ho, The Rain
content:
The Lark that in heaven dim
     Can match a rainy hour
     With his own music's shower,
    Can make me sing like him,

title: The Truth
content:
Since I have seen a bird one day,
    His head pecked more than half away;
    That hopped about, with but one eye,
    Ready to fight again, a

title: Young Beauty
content:
When at each door the ruffian winds
     Have laid a dying man to groan,
    And filled the air on winter nights
     With cries of infants lef

title: A Greeting
content:
Good morning, Life, and all
    Things glad and beautiful.
    My pockets nothing hold,
    But he that owns the gold,
    The Sun, is my grea

title: When Yon Full Moon
Night Wanderers
content:
They hear the bell of midnight toll,
    And shiver in their flesh and soul;
    They lie on hard, cold wood or stone,

title: On Hearing Mrs. Woodhouse Play The Harpsichord
content:
We poets pride ourselves on what
     We feel, and not what we achieve;
    The world may call our children fools,
     Enough for us that we c

title: A Strange City
content:
A wondrous city, that had temples there
    More rich than that one built by David's son,
    Which called forth Ophir's gold, when Israel

title: Sweet Stay-At-Home
content:
Sweet Stay-at-Home, sweet Well-content,
    Thou knowest of no strange continent:
    Thou hast not felt thy bosom keep
    A gentle motion wit

title: Dreams Of The Sea
content:
I know not why I yearn for thee again,
     To sail once more upon thy fickle flood;
    I'll hear thy waves wash under my death-bed,
     Thy

title: Poor Kings
content:
God's pity on poor kings,
        They know no gentle rest;
    The North and South cry out,
        Cries come from East and West, 
    "Come

title: When On A Summer's Morn
content:
When on a summer's morn I wake,
     And open my two eyes,
    Out to the clear, born-singing rills
     My bird-like spirit flies,

    To he

title: In May
content:
Yes, I will spend the livelong day
    With Nature in this month of May;
    And sit beneath the trees, and share
    My bread with birds who

title: Children At Play
content:
I hear a merry noise indeed:
     Is it the geese and ducks that take
    Their first plunge in a quiet pond
     That into scores of ripples b

title: The Old Oak Tree
content:
I sit beneath your leaves, old oak,
     You mighty one of all the trees;
    Within whose hollow trunk a man
     Could stable his big horse w

title: The Kingfisher
content:
It was the Rainbow gave thee birth,
     And left thee all her lovely hues;
    And, as her mother's name was Tears,
     So runs it in thy b

title: Love's Inspiration
content:

     Thy thoughts of me, like worms this day,

title: A Child's Pet
content:

     But bleated o'er th

title: The Bird Of Paradise
content:
Here comes Kate Summers, who, for gold,
     Takes any man to bed:
    "You knew my friend, Nell Barnes," she said;
     "You knew Nell Barnes

title: The Mind's Liberty
content:
The mind, with its own eyes and ears,
     May for these others have no care;
    No matter where this body is,
     The mind is free to go els

title: Winter's Beauty
content:

    When leaves are born, and hear birds sing?
    And when they lose their singing powers,

title: When The Cuckoo Sings
content:

     And clouds like greater moons can shine;
    When every leafy tree doth hold

title: The Wonder Maker
content:

title: Hidden Love
content:

    But captured, soon grows dumb; and we,
    To hear his fast declining powers,

title: The Church Organ
content:
